page: Microsoft Azure M32ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz and includes 38 MB of L3 cache. The server is equipped with 875.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 27.34 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 1049 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct tradeoff between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the weak and poor categories, which is also reflected in poor single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds. However, the 32 vCPUs deliver average multi-core performance across stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds, although the overall Passmark memory score is classified as weak.

Given its massive memory capacity of 875.0 GB relative to its 32 vCPUs, the M32ms is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require leading-edge single-thread compute speeds. It is well-suited for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy caching layers like Redis, and enterprise database operations. The server also supports moderate multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation and medium-sized language model inference, but is less optimal for single-threaded processing or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure M32ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server features a massive 875.0 GB of RAM, translating to 27.34 GB per core, alongside 1049 GB of local storage. While single-core benchmark performance is weak, the system delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. This hardware profile makes the M32ms highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, such as large databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications, where memory capacity is prioritized over raw single-threaded CPU speed.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M32ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 875.0 GB of RAM, and 1049 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring massive RAM capacity and dedicated Intel processors for enterprise database and caching workloads.

Massive 875.0 GB of system memory providing 27.34 GB per core
Includes 1049 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core performance balanced by weaker single-core execution
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise caching layers
